short_reason: Strong, well-structured article with excellent keyword integration and clear practical value. The language-specific implementations (JS, Python, Go) and the common bug patterns section are standout practical additions. The quick reference table and security implications sections add real depth. Recommend minor polish only — the padding explanation in the Go section could be clarified.
red_flags: The Go encoding example could clarify that `base64.RawURLEncoding` produces unpadded output by default — this may confuse readers familiar with standard Base64 behavior
The security implications section is comparatively brief; the 'signature bypass' claim could be illustrated with a concrete code example
The JWT example signature appears to be truncated placeholder text rather than a real signature
